Memory management and debugging tools, build scripts, licenses, and documentation for the DUMA library.


## Files
- **[COPYING-GPL](COPYING-GPL.md)**: The GNU General Public License Version 2, June 1991, detailing terms for software distribution and modification.
- **[COPYING-LGPL](COPYING-LGPL.md)**: The GNU Lesser General Public License Version 2.1, February 1999, for software libraries.
- **[duma.c](duma.c.md)**: A special version of `malloc()` for debugging memory overruns and underruns by using inaccessible memory pages.
- **[duma.h](duma.h.md)**: Header file for the DUMA memory allocator, providing macros and functions for memory management and debugging.
- **[duma_config.h](duma_config.h.md)**: Configuration settings for DUMA, including memory management and initialization options.
- **[duma_hlp.h](duma_hlp.h.md)**: Helper functions for managing memory slots in the DUMA Red-Zone memory allocator.
- **[duma_sem.h](duma_sem.h.md)**: Internal header file for semaphore operations in the DUMA memory allocator with optional thread safety.
- **[LICENSE](LICENSE.md)**: GNU Lesser General Public License Version 2.1, February 1999, for software libraries and programs.
- **[noduma.h](noduma.h.md)**: Header file for removing previous DUMA and standard C/C++ function definitions and wrappers.
- **[paging.h](paging.h.md)**: System and platform-dependent paging functions for memory allocation and protection.
- **[print.c](print.c.md)**: Internal implementation of aborting and printing functions for the DUMA memory allocator.
- **[print.h](print.h.md)**: Internal header file for aborting and printing functions without using stdio in a memory allocator.
- **[README.txt](README.txt.md)**: Documentation for DUMA, a tool to detect memory access errors by using virtual memory hardware.
- **[sem_inc.c](sem_inc.c.md)**: Implements thread safety functions using semaphores for the DUMA memory allocator.
- **[wscript](wscript.md)**: Build script for the DUMA library with platform-specific configurations and conditional compilation.
